Name: Dianna H.
Comment:
It pains me to give this restaurant 3 stars since it's a small mom and pop joint, but all the food felt extra oily, they got our order wrong, and we all thought the service could be improved.
I needed to make a relatively last minute reservation for Mother's Day lunch, and I was pleasantly surprised that LoveMama had plenty of slots available just a couple days beforehand.
When my party of 4 arrived on Sunday at 11:30, I was surprised to see the whole restaurant was empty. We were the first party seated. It gradually filled up, but it definitely wasn't nearly as busy as I thought it'd be for a restaurant in this location with so many reviews on Yelp.
Maybe it's because we came on a rainy day, but most of the restaurant felt very dim even at 11:30 am. We sat at the table closest to the window for maximum light. 
They offer a good selection of Thai, Vietnamese, and Malaysian appetizers and entrees at a very affordable price point. I was particularly impressed by their $12 weekday lunch combo, which comes with one appetizer + entree.
I shared with one other friend and the rest got their own food. We shared:
Roti canai planta ($7) - this is the first time I didn't love roti canai at a restaurant. The roti just felt extra oily and the lentil dip wasn't that tasty
Rendang nasi lemak with chicken ($15) - this is their most popular dish, so I knew we had to try it. The owner took our order and recommended we get it with chicken instead of beef, which is the usual meat. The chicken came with a lot of flavor, but we could barely taste the coconut in the coconut rice, and there wasn't nearly enough rice to go with the amount of chicken provided
Char kwa teow with beef (but not sure if we got pad see eew instead??) - we asked for this with beef and not spicy, and the first time we got the dish, it came with chili flakes all over it and had chicken. I would've let the chicken slide, but I'm a wimp when it comes to spice and one bite of the dish had my mouth burning, so we let the owner know and he quickly came back with a non-spicy beef noodle dish. All's well that ends well, right....
HOWEVER, when we checked the bill at the end of the meal, we realized he entered Pad See Eew into their ordering system, so I'm pretty sure we got pad see eew rather than char kwa teow. Very disappointed that they got the order wrong. No wonder the noodles were on the sweeter side; I was very confused by the sweetness.
My two other friends ordered the pad thai and both enjoyed their noodles. I think that's the dish to get here.
topics:
- Category: Food Quality
  - Description: The food felt extra oily, the roti canai was not tasty, the coconut rice didn't have enough coconut flavor, and the pad see eew was mistakenly served instead of char kwa teow.
  - Attitude: Dissatisfied

Name: Salina L.
Comment:
I went to LoveMama for dinner. The owner greeted us warmly when we first walked in.
The first thing I noticed was how dim the lighting was in the restaurant. Though it is nice to have dimmer lighting, it was too dark to the point where we couldn't see the menu or our food clearly. I wish the lighting could be brighter at night, so we can appreciate the presentation of our food. (The photos seem brighter because of the iPhone camera's night mode feature).
As for food, my friend and I ordered the chicken satay to share. The chicken satay was really good and I would definitely recommend ordering it. I also ordered the shrimp laksa. The soup was really comforting and sufficiently spicy. What I was not a fan of was the shrimp. From the texture, it didn't seem very fresh. I also was surprised that the noodles were hard to chew/stiff. The noodles were either undercooked or had been left out for too long before being cooked.
Despite these factors, I would still return to try other things on the menu because the flavors of the dishes I tried were good!

Name: Angelita P.
Comment: I will forever recommend this restaurant to everyone who comes to visit New York as the BEST PAD THAI ever! This restaurant incorporates Malaysian, Vietnamese and Thai influenced Southeast Asian cuisines in their menu, and even with all this diversity, EVERY dish tastes authentic and so flavorful!! It's a small and cozy restaurant located in the East Village with ambient lighting and excellent service every time. You can go in there all dressed up before a night out in the city, or you can go in comfy sweatpants, regardless you will always feel welcomed by the servers. It's a very laid back spot and all your meals will come on plastic plates, so please keep in mind this isn't fancy! I genuinely can't brag about this place enough. Some of the dishes I highly recommend: for starters my absolute favorite is the Roti Canai Planta which is basically like this delicious, flat, flaky, and warm dough that has a similar consistency to a croissant, with a curry style dipping sauce on the side. So simple, but it's one of the items I look forward to the most. Other great appetizers are the Bao Buns and Uncles dumplings. For the main course, once again, the PAD THAI! I personally get it with shrimp and ask for it spicy because I love the kick. The red curry is also phenomenal. I can just go on and on about this place but it won't do justice until you try it out! I truly hope you do! :)

Name: Sherene N.
Comment:
No frills, no nonsense. Superior authentic Thai/Vietnamese/Malaysian food. Yesterday was sibling day, and we always venture out to new and beloved spots. After our massages, we were famished, and my sister was craving Thai while I was craving Vietnamese.  This popped up in a subcategory on the Yelp search. Immediately, it grabbed my attention because, could this be? Exactly what I'm searching for? Yes. Yes, it was. A mash up of all things good, and we got to trekking. Small spot, fast service. Attentive. 
Decor was on the lower end, but to me, this signals that the food can hold its own. So, I was excited. 2 Thai iced teas, wings, Thai Tom yum soup, curry coconut soup, Uncle Phats dumplings, and a honey porkchop. The three of us simultaneously agreed the honey porkchop was the best thing on the table and of course it was mine and they'd wished they'd went with my pick lol But, I'm glad they didn't. We picked at each other's dishes. The soups were so authentic and fresh that it was mind-blowing. Served in a simple understated take out container. We were pleasantly surprised at the complexity of flavors. 
The table favorite was the honey porkchop. Served with rice and palette cleansing veggies. Grilled to perfection,  charred beautifully. Marinated somewhere between Vietnamese and Malaysian style. Absolutely delicious. Uncle Phats dumplings came in a peanut sauce that elevated it to another level. You can tell that whatever comes out of the kitchen isn't coming from a frozen bag. It is very fresh, and if it is frozen, they've perfected their techniques on how to appear freshly made. The dumplings itself were made so that you can taste the meat, the green onion, garlic, and slight ginger. 
The wings were perfectly cooked and sauced to perfection in a general tso type of sauce. 
The service was good. Nothing ostentatious or annoying. We shared plates, but nobody tripped and no added fee either. The entire meal came out to roughly $63. Before leaving, we met the owner who popped out and asked if we liked everything, and we quickly sung their praises in unison. My sibling are not easy to impress. We come from a land of food diversity and we often know what can be proclaimed as authentic and who's faking it. There is no faking here. This is all the way true to the game. We will definitely be back to try other things. Loved everything we got. Much love to the owners.

Name: Matthew S.
Comment:
Soul food is something I enjoy. The fried dishes, the fact I can eat it any time of day, and there will always be something on the menu I can find. Generous portions, even more generous prices. Jovial employees, and an idea that you ate something that while may not be great for your body, but good for the soul. Hence the cuisine.
Unfortunately for Amy Ruth's, some of these guidelines do not apply, or perhaps passed me by. The prices are fairly high, the portions are fairly small, and the food is generally mediocre, like really middle of the road compared to the other options in the area. Perhaps that explains why it was not terribly busy during dinner.
To be as fair as possible, I will break this review into one of my report card reviews so to not leave anything out.
--
Service: Professional, courteous, genuine. My server, Tashi, was on her game and had great knowledge of the menu. Unfortunately she needs help. They seem to lack staff here, and it shows as the employees look exhausted from head to toe. Grade: A-
Atmosphere: Maybe it was the air conditioning unit, or something else, but it always sounded like elephants were walking over my head. It made the experience unpleasant and fairly jarring. There is a minimum purchase limit if you want to pay with a credit card. Overall, not impressed. Grade: D+
Food: What really makes up the overall score of this review. Along with the complementary cornbread, I got a Sunrise, Fried Catfish, a Chicken and Waffles, and some macaroni and cheese. All be told, the food was generally mediocre, save for the catfish, which was first rate. Grade: C
--
Final thoughts: Close by the subway, a traditional 1920's vibe inside, and a menu with items named after famous patrons (and others), it feels more touristy than heartfelt. It was worth trying; now I have no urge to return. As for you, the reader, go try Red Rooster, which is nearby, for a more desirable outcome.

This review comes as my second time with a horrible experience from this restaurant. I placed an order online totaling $84 and when I came to pick it up, I called the the Caucasian manager (or owner) on the phone from across the room and he claimed that they were not taking orders online. Instead of just being honest and saying that they are busy and the order was not ready, why tell a lie? When I stepped to him, he was like "oh did we confirm it? Oh it's not ready yet." Again this is not the first time they messed up with me. 
The first time they messed up was when I ordered a Thanksgiving turkey from them with stuffing, which costed over $100. The turkey was cut up and had greens in it, and the stuffing was stove top with cranberries in it and about as dry as the Sahara. The turkey was also swimming in butter. They are under new ownership and clearly have forgotten who their core demographic of customers are. I will never patronize this restaurant again. Complete trash. The food is not giving what it once gave.

Comment:
Amy Ruth's unquestionably takes the crown for serving the best banana pudding in the tri-state area, and I'm willing to travel to Harlem time and again to indulge in this heavenly dessert. From the moment you take your first bite, you'll understand why it's worth the journey. Ordering over the phone is a breeze, thanks to their swift and efficient service. The staff is friendly and accommodating, making the process easy and stress-free. And when it's time to pick up your order, you'll be in and out in no time flat, thanks to their fast and seamless process.
But let's talk about the star of the night - the banana pudding. Creamy, dreamy, and utterly irresistible, it's a dessert that will transport you to dessert paradise with every spoonful. The perfect balance of flavors and textures leaves you craving more, long after the last bite is gone.
Look no further than Amy Ruth's. Whether you're a local or just passing through, it's a dessert destination that's well worth the trip.

Name: Darren D.
Comment: Had my son place an online order for me to pickup on my way home. I got there well before order pickup time. As I pulled up in front of AR a rare thing was available, yes it was a parking spot. Figured I'd park and do some work on my laptop. A min later a young lady pulled up next to me and asked if I was leaving. I said "yes in 15 min when my food is ready". She said "ok I'll wait". I thought about it, and realized she might live in the area or might have dinner plans etc. so I backed up into the bus stop so she could park. After she parked and thanked me  she asked my name.. 10 min later she emerged with my order... curb side service with a beautiful smile!!Couldn't thank her enough... Ms T.J. That was great- truly a nice gesture. O yeah the food was 5/5 like it always is .. Kindness begets Kindness. Great Employee

Name: Jim E.
Comment:
This was some of the worst soul food I've ever had. I saw so many reviews saying this place is better than Red Rooster and Sylvia's and I couldn't disagree more. 
Salmon Croquettes- 7/10 
Corn bread- 4/10. These were quite dry. Nothing special, could have made it better at home.
Fried chicken- 1/10. This was the driest, most flavorless fried chicken I have ever had, was appalled by this dish. Royally screwed up a staple dish.
Mac- 4/10, again lackluster in flavor. Have had way better. 
Jerk chicken- 7/10. Was pretty solid, chicken was juicy and well cooked. Jerk sauce was alright but not on par with a Jamaican spot.
Collards- 9/10. Easily the best thing we had. Super flavorful, very well seasoned.
Cheesy grits- 8/10. These were solid but needed a bit of salt
Yams- 6/10. Not bad but nothing special.

My friend and I came for brunch. We were served warm cornbread, which was buttery and moist. I ordered the Waffle and Fried Pork Chop. The waffle and nicely crunchy and fluffy. The pork chop was perfectly crispy, tender, as well as wonderfully juicy.

Name: Shelly J.
Comment:
I love fried chicken and waffles and it's like the best combo. I've been to many but in NYC (Harlem) this place is theeeee best. Now to get here is no cake walk...  you can take the subway, if you dare, or UBER. 
It's a little hard to find cuz their signage is hard to find.  
This would have been a 5 star review. As I entered, no one greeted or welcome us.
This one grumpy older man was sitting down. Heavy set, kinda tall, sitting down on a stool.
, I thought he was waiting for his food,  or for someone. While we are figuring out where to go to sit down. This fool looked at us and gesture with his hands and eyes like what?! 
I was like oh we are here to sit down.  I don't know what his deal was. I hope that wasn't the owner. Cuz he needs to get rid of the attitude. 
They give you complimentary corn bread.
Very disappointed. It lacks so much. A cornbread from a Jiffy box, tastes better. 
The menu has entrees named after famous people. I ordered, the AL Sharpton.  Chicken and Waffles.  
Get their 50/50. Iced Tea n Lemonade.  Expensive but it's worth it. 
My waffle was amazing ... chicken was good... I didn't ordered much , cuz I ate a lot at Juniors.
Ok so I've been to Melbas which is also in Harlem, and this place is better. A bit expensive but service was good.  Leaving the restaurant,  I thought Mr. Gramps
would of changed his bad attitude, or thanked his patrons, you know they ones who keeps the place going.  But no. No thank you, or have a nice afternoon.  None of that.  The wait staff is friendly though

Name: Andrew A.
Comment:
3.89 Stars is my actual score. 
So here's the thing.... I've eaten at this fine historic establishment before. I believe it was maybe 2 years ago or 1. I can't honestly remember what I had previously. But I remember enjoying it. 
But anyways my review still stands for it to be a 3.89 etc etc etc. only because I just enjoy my chicken with alil but a spice to it especially when it's fried..
Let me paint a picture for you readers. I've a HUGE FAN OF CHICKEN AND WAFFLES ever since it first tasted my lips many moons ago. So I literally became OBSESSED (on the Mariah Carey scale) with the combination. So everywhere and anywhere I went (that I would think served CHICKEN AND WAFFLES) I would need to have it at least once a month! The last time I had it at another establishment it was truly disappointing. IT WAS CHICKEN F**$k*^G TENDERS!! The fried crust falling off the meat. Extremely burnt and just straight up horrible portion. 
BACK TO MS AMY RUTH's now I must say the portion PERFECT, HOSPITALITY (SUPERB) the entire staff was friendly and professional. The bathroom (decent)! The seating was good! The entirety felt good and great. My meal.... So I ordered the chicken and waffle combo (I forgot the actual name of the meal) but I added bananas and cinnamon my waffles (with and order of cheesy grits and collard greens). You're given one complimentary cornbread (which was warm and a bit flaky) but I enjoyed it. I added (cold) syrup (already on the table) with one packet of butter. But felt like it was missing something though.. Split the cornbread as if it was burger buns and enjoyed it...savored it. Now for my meal.... I was always never a fan of grits because I just thought it was gross. Can't lie about that. Barely ordered it, had a bad experience one time; never again! But here at AMY RUTH's I did honestly enjoy it but it didn't look the same as any other grits I've seen before. I mean I tasted the cheese but I felt like it was missing something. I'm no grits whisperer but I gave it a chance ! I enjoyed it. The collard greens was too damn hot to eat at one point (plus the fried chicken) so I had to let it cook down a bit. Like was it near molten lava?! Now I know everything is cooked fresher etc etc; but damn! Now the waffles tasted good with alil extra extra extra times 2 drizzle of cold syrup. Which made it taste better. I enjoyed it.  The fried chicken had the perfect texture and crunch to it...but damn I love my fried chicken with extra spice to it! It was missing something! The portion everything was right but everything was missing something yet for some reason I do plan on returning... it's just the damn price isn't definitely right. Maybe because I added two sides. But to me honestly the price... the meal was just missing something! But I do plan on returning! Thank you for the hospitality! Also my condolences to the founder... I noticed the sign on the wall inside. 
Please support the locals !

Name: Helen C.
Comment:
I'm a native New Yorker and was super excited to visit Amy Ruth's. It was this past Saturday afternoon around 4pm. As my friend and I were being seated, a male employee made a sourpuss facial expression. It was unfriendly and it was a red flag - micro aggression at it's finest.
Our female server with short hair, parted to the side and hair tied back in a small ponytail or bun was nice, until I asked a question, so maybe it was fake nice. It was my first time at Amy Ruth's, I inquired about the fried chicken and honey friend chicken and the server immediately reacted with irritation. I was surprised by her reaction and found it abusive. She works there and knows the food well and reluctantly provided a slither of feedback. I  decided to go with the basic fried chicken. Then, she asked me if I wanted white meat or dark meat. I was confused, I thought it would be maybe a mix of fried chicken pieces. I was afraid and also turned-off to ask another menu-related question, so I blurted out white meat. Immediately, her face frowned with judgement, supreme dissatisfaction, as she swiveled her head with attitude -- I guess she did not like my answer? Why was she so judgmental and prejudiced against my answer? Was it because she would have chosen the dark meat chicken? It's a chicken and you're not the one eating or paying for it.
She asked if we wanted drinks and my friend said the ice tea. I was quiet - I felt unwelcome and uncomfortable. She looked at me with a frown and as she was already marching her bobble head away, she made time to sarcastically say, "you're good with water???".... This server was truly the epitome of an adult bully with the attitude of a gassy infant. I was so turned off, regretted being there - I hated it.
So my dish arrived and it's literally half a hen - it reminded me of rotisserie chicken from Boston Market. If the server had patience for basic menu-related questions, I truly believe that this experience would have turned out differently.
My friend who had the dark meat chicken cut her chicken open and it was bleeding... "bloody Mary!" Bak bak bak. The server took the bleeding chicken back and by the time I was almost finished with my dish is when my friend's chicken was served - this time, it was fully cooked but super dry........I said, "probably taste like chicken breast."
When it was time for the check, we saw the servers for the other tables but ours was MIA. We waited, perhaps, 10-15 minutes. In conclusion, I found the server myself and requested for the check myself -- I guess you have to do things yourself when you pay 20% tip, tip, tip, tip, bak, bak, bak.
I'd like to give a shout out to the nice hispanic man that brought our food to the table - he was friendly, kind and treated me like a human being. Unfortunately, the server and the other male worker who made a face as we were being seated, did not seem to want me there. Those who have experienced prejudice know. It's incredibly disappointing when people of color bully other people of color.
The corn bread, mac and cheese, collard greens and waffle were delicious. I wasn't happy with the chicken cause all I tasted was the b*tchy server being b*tchy to me. Unfortunately, my dining experience was hijacked by a hostile, prejudiced server who clearly is prejudiced against white CHICKEN meat. I mean....YOUR establishment offers the option between white or dark meat CHICKEN and YOU asked me, but then have a issue with the answer? I'm not going to share a photo of my fried chicken, just to be petty. PS- I'm Asian and clearly they had qualms about my white CHICKEN meat! I felt unwelcome in THEIR establishment and in THEIR neighborhood. How hypocritical. Get over yourself, bunch of haters.

Name: Brenda L.
Comment:
My experience at Amy Ruth's was rather underwhelming, especially for it being the "#2 best chicken and waffles" in the country.
The space seems homey and well loved, upon walking in there's a big entry space. We weren't greeted and were a tad bit confused on whether it was a order before and seat yourself, or wait for the host to seat you.
The waitress we had was sweet, and I had the chance to try a few items:
- Mac N Cheese (2/5): This reminded me of eating mac and cheese during Thanksgiving dinner, after it sitting out for 8+ hours lol. ai wasn't a fan of the texture and it just tasted fairly bland and dry in general.
- Chicken & Waffles (3/5): The waffle was good! We asked for dark meat though (they let you choose dark or white) and it was maybe 80% white meat and 20% dark meat. The chicken was well seasoned but some parts of the chicken didn't maintain its crispness.
- Cornbread (3/5): Complimentary before you eat! Definitely douse it in butter because it was much needed from the dryness. The bread was fluffy and nice.
- Grits (2/5): Also very bland :( We took a bite and immediately did not enjoy it at all.
In general, the food was a rough pass for us and I'm wishing that we visited Sylvia's instead.

Name: A M.
Comment:
Our experience at this place was rather disappointing, especially considering the high expectations we had for their claimed southern cuisine. From the moment we entered the restaurant, we were underwhelmed. The overall upkeep of the place seemed lacking, and the service was far from welcoming, with slow response times. The layout of the establishment also contributed to a less-than-ideal atmosphere.
We decided to try their chicken and waffles, which they boldly claimed to be the second best in the country on their website. However, it failed to make a lasting impression. The fried chicken was rather basic, lacking any standout flavors that would set it apart from other establishments. The waffle, while not bad, was also unremarkable. Additionally, we ordered a side of chicken wings, mac and cheese, and coleslaw. The chicken wings were average in taste, not offering anything particularly unique. The mac and cheese was overly simple, with overcooked pasta and unremarkable cheddar cheese. The coleslaw fared slightly better in comparison but remained quite standard.
Overall, the experience left much to be desired. As a supposed Harlem staple boasting the best Southern Cuisine, it failed to distinguish itself. If you happen to be craving southern food and live within walking distance, this place might be passable. However, if you reside outside of the neighborhood, you can easily find comparable options at your local southern restaurant. We'll give it a three-star rating primarily because the staff kindly held and returned my girlfriend's misplaced purse when we went to retrieve it.

Name: Stacie B.
Comment:
I took my mom there on  Mother's day weekend.
The fried chicken had no seasoning, and it was so huge it must have been feed steroids.  My chicken was overcooked and difficult to chew. The  turkey wings were big and not done.  A cheap grade of turkey wings.  The skin of the turkey wing was nasty.  Chewing the turkey wings was like chewing rubber.  We had to cook the wings some more.    If you don't have the time to throughly cook turkey wings don't sell them.  My ribs were burnt.  They were not done.  Some folks ribs had to be cooked some more..  The potatoe salad had a nice texture but no seasoning.  The sweet potato pie was thick like a cement mix.  I would not give a child a slice of that pie.  If you ran out of milk you should have stop making the pies.  The bannana pudding was good.  I did not like their dry cornbread.
I was embarrassed to have taken my mom to this restaurant, and I won't be returning. The chef that cooked that day needs to take a few  lessons on how to cook soul food.  I went the Saturday before mothers day.  
Go to Sylvia's instead.

Name: Connie H.
Comment:
I really wanted to have soul food in Harlem and this place was highly recommended even amidst all the great choices in the area. Honestly, it delivered and I had a positive experience here.
Loves:
+ Good food (I had the Reggie Harris and loved it)
+ Fun names (eg. President Barack Obama, Gabrielle Union, Ludacris, Michelle Obama)
+ Generous portion sizes so you will def have leftovers
+ Food is quick to come out and served hot
+ Complimentary cornbread
While the prices seem a bit high, you do get a lot of food and will have leftovers so there's that. Also I believe the space (eg. chairs, tables) can get more modern but I do appreciate the artwork all around.
Thanks for the good food Amy Ruth's, and I hope to be back sooner than later to try the waffles!

LT*

Name: Tamika M.
Comment:
I love this place. Came here for dinner to celebrate my daughters graduation. Highly recommend reservations if you are planning to dine inside the restaurant part. This place gets really packed inside or out. There is a small parking garage for L&B customers only. 
This place is known for their Sicilian square pizza. We dined inside. We decided not to eat pizza for tonight. Instead, we ordered appetizers and entrees. For appetizer, we ordered fried shrimp and mozzarella sticks. Fried shrimp are big and delicious. Super tasty with a squeeze of lemon on it, and the tartar sauce they serve it with is delicious. Mozzarella sticks were nice and cheesy. You definitely get that cheese pull effect.  
For entrees, we ordered chicken parmigiana, penne with vodka sauce with grilled chicken, chicken Caesar salad,  calamari with linguini red sauce, penne with grilled chicken in pesto sauce, burger basket with French fries. Chicken Caesar salad was delicious. The romaine lettuce was nice and crisp, and the grilled chicken was seasoned and cooked very well. The penne in vodka sauce was nice and creamy, mushrooms are added, and peas. Penne with grilled chicken in pesto sauce was one of my favorites. The pesto sauce was delicious. The calamari in red sauce was really good, and it had tons of calamari. Chicken parmigiana was super tender and cheesy. The burger basket was good. Everything we had was packing with flavors, and the portion sizes are huge. Easily, three people can eat from one pasta dish. 
For dessert, everyone wanted ice cream. We ordered chocolate, cookies & cream, creamscicle, and strawberry ice-cream flavors. My favorite is the creamscicle, but all are rich in flavor.   
I highly recommend you try this place. There's is something on the menu for everyone. Staff is super friendly and attentive. Service is quick. The food is delicious.

Name: Rebecca M.
Comment:
We are longtime fans (obviously) of the Sicilian slice here which is famous for very good reason, but hadn't actually eaten in the restaurant itself til tonight. Wow -- when it comes to traditional red sauce Italian, this is among the best we've ever had.
First of all: it's actually open late!!!! It's so refreshing these days to find a place where you can actually sit down and eat truly until midnight -- and the scene both in and outside was very much bustling when we arrived at about 10'pm on a Wednesday. 
Service is fast, friendly and the food is all SUPER fresh. We had the meatballs, the Caesar salad and the shrimp & mussels fra diavolo on spaghetti. Everything tastes totally made to order. There are many mediocre Caesar salads in the world but this is not one -- huge, super crisp romaine, zesty and creamy homemade dressing. The spaghetti was the real pleasant surprise. Tons of big shrimp and mussels all perfectly cooked; the pasta itself in a flavorful sauce full of fresh basil and also cooked to perfection.
Of course had to pick up a spumoni to go as well. So great to know there is a lot more to love here besides the famous pizza!

Name: Joseph D.
Comment:
L&B Spumoni Gardens: A Brooklyn Legacy Serving Pizza and Spumoni Since 1918
L&B Spumoni Gardens in Brooklyn is a sacred destination for any pizza lover. It's not just a pizzeria; it's a slice of history that began in 1918 when the founder started selling pizza from a horse-drawn wagon--the "food truck" of its day. By 1939, he set down roots by purchasing property in Brooklyn to establish a spumoni factory. For those unfamiliar, spumoni is an Italian dessert made by mixing layers of chocolate, vanilla, and pistachio ice cream--a trifecta of flavors that became a neighborhood sensation.
As his popularity for pizza and spumoni soared, he added an outdoor seating area and a community gathering spot was born. The evolution continued, and by 1950, he built the pizza shop, serving the now-famous Sicilian pizza, a marvel of culinary craft: the mother dough (masa madre), a spongy, thick dough with a chewy, cloud-like texture achieved by layering mozzarella directly on the dough, then topping it with a rich tomato sauce and a dusting of Parmesan after it comes out of the oven. Seeing these hot, sizzling pies being knife-cut into squares as you approach the front of the line adds to the excitement of the deliciousness you're about to experience. While all the slices have a beautiful blend of crispy and chewy textures, I always ask for those extra crispy corner slices.
L&B remains family-owned, with staff that greets everyone with a smile and ensures your experience is as memorable as the food. Over a lifetime of visits, I've never had a bad experience--a testament to their consistency and care.
Today, the line at L&B is a testament to its enduring appeal, reminiscent of the days when eager locals lined up at the original wagon. It's one of the few lines I'll gladly join, with the promise of pizza that is among the best in the world. The ice cream window, where spumoni is king, offers an assortment of amazing handmade ice cream and Italian ice; my favorite ice flavors are cherry and rainbow. 
They also have a full sit-down restaurant offering a full menu of traditional Italian fare. With parking available next door (though it fills up quickly), plenty of street parking, and ample outdoor seating, dining at L&B is an essential Brooklyn experience. Under the open skies of the City of Churches, L&B Spumoni Gardens stands as a cathedral of culinary history, continuing to serve up slices of joy to everyone who steps through its doors.

Once seated, we ordered the Sicilian Square Pizza with mushrooms on top. The pizza exceeded our expectations, with the perfect balance of thickness and crispiness that we both enjoyed. Despite this minor critique, we both agreed that L & B Spumoni Gardens offers exceptional pizza, and we would gladly return for another meal.

Name: Laura W.
Comment:
L&B will always be a special place for me. It's a hike to get out to, nuts it's absolutely worth it. You can order at the window or go inside the pizzeria and sit down. There's cafeteria styles seating outside, which is great during the summer. You'll go to a separate window if you want spumoni. In all my years of going here I haven't ate at the sit down side of the restaurant, but I've heard it's very good Italian food and you can also order a fresh pie to your table.
My tip is to get there early around opening time. Grab a few slices to sit down and eat, a couple for the car on the ride home (trust me, you will be craving the next slice after you finished what's on your plate), and make sure to grab a pie or two for later that day. I usually freeze some too. They reheat very well at 400 degrees for 5-7 min. 
Don't forget to grab a spumoni too. Is it the best? You decide, but it makes for a full experience. I'll tell you this though, you cannot talk about great NY pizza without having L&B in the convo. In my opinion, it's in the Top 5.

I went back and had a creamsicle soft serve which was much more to my liking. So, good option for a Sicilian slice if you find yourself way down by Coney Island. Just not sure I would make a special trip all the way out there.

Name: Jay B.
Comment:
Culinary Heaven in Brooklyn
L&B Spumoni Gardens is not just a pizzeria; it's a cherished slice of Italy nestled in the heart of Brooklyn. The moment you step into this family-owned gem, you're transported to an era where every meal felt like an event.
The incredible Italian experience they offer is more than just incredible pizza; it's a testament to their commitment to tradition and outstanding service. Speaking of pizza, if heaven existed on earth, it would undoubtedly be in their sauce, and the Sicilian pie is the divine manifestation of that. The crust, a perfect balance of crispy and doughy, supports a layer of sauce that's nothing short of pure culinary magic.
The ambiance at L&B Spumoni Gardens has only improved over time. Stepping into this establishment feels like a journey into history, where each visit becomes a chapter in the story of a family legacy. It's not just a meal; it's an experience, an immersion into the rich tapestry of Italian culinary heritage.
And here's a pro tip: Save room for dessert. The Spumoni is not just a treat; it's an art form, and when prepared for a group, it becomes a sculpted masterpiece. This family knows how to turn a meal into an event, creating memories that linger long after the last bite.
There are few places in NYC that I love more than L&B Spumoni Gardens. Bring friends, make an event out of it, and savor every moment because this pizzeria is a treasure trove of flavors and traditions that capture the essence of culinary bliss.
topics:
- Category: Food Quality
  - Description: The Sicilian pie with an incredible sauce and perfect balance of crispy and doughy crust.
  - Attitude: Satisfied

- Category: Ambiance
  - Description: The ambiance that feels like a journey into history and a chapter in a family legacy.

- Category: Value for Money

The slice - so good. Consistent throughout all the years I've been coming here. The sauce is delicious, the dough is crispy but soft in the inside and the cheese is under the sauce. It's really good.
The Spumoni ice cream is also a must order! They make it in house and it's delicious.

Name: Jason V.
Comment:
Vastly overrated, WAY overpriced. First off, I get there and like many others, I'm taken aback by the scene from the iconic movie, "Serendipity." But they don't care, they treat you like just another person, and the hostess could care less about why you're there or what you want. There are literally 15 open tables, but they won't let me upstairs. She asks for a phone number and I nicely ask why? She says she needs it for a reservation. I don't need a reservation, you guys are slow as heck at 2pm. Regardless, I sit down and you are immediately taken aback by the $18 minimum purchase. Like you can't even split a sundae with a date. You have to order a $18 ice cream. I'm ready to gorge myself, I am waiting patiently to order, but I hear a conversation behind me. Waitress comes by and grabs a plate from the table (pre-bussing). The older lady then says, "excuse me, but I'm not done yet. Why would you just grab my plate." Waitress: in a very rude tone, "I'm doing my job. You clearly are done." 
So instead of apologizing and going about her job, she continues to argue with this 75-80 yr old lady. Then comes to me and says, "what do you want to order?" Not a "hello," or "hi my name is..." Nothing. I basically excused myself and lost all respect for this restaurant and what you now stand for. It's no longer about the customer and the ambiance of this iconic place, it's about making money, rude waitresses just like everywhere else in NYC, and not giving a damn about people wanting to come back.

Name: Daniel M.
Comment:
Serendipity 3 is where your sweet tooth meets its wildest dreams, and your diet comes to die a glorious death. I walked in thinking I'd just have a little dessert and walked out questioning my life choices--mainly, why I don't live here.
The Frozen Hot Chocolate is a paradox wrapped in a conundrum, served in a goblet big enough to swim in. I considered using a straw, but a lifeguard might have been more appropriate. The desserts are so over-the-top, they make Mount Everest look like a molehill.
The decor is as quirky as your Aunt Mildred's attic, but way more charming and less dusty. Service is great, even if they don't help you roll out the door afterward. If you're in New York and looking for a sugar rush and a good laugh, Serendipity 3 is the place to be!

Name: Ed U.
Comment:
My perennial question about this NYC institution has been if this is Serendipity 3, where exactly are Serendipity 1 and 2? Surely I can't be the only knucklehead to have this question. According to Google Search, the answer is that the '3' refers to the restaurant's trio of founders. Hmm. I also read Selena Gomez has a current ownership stake. Girl gets around.
Everyone knows about Serendipity 3. Although I had been to the Miami and Vegas locations in the past, it's been ages since my last visit here. It was such a hot day I thought it might be amusing for Jamie W. and me to go after traipsing and sweltering in Sheep Meadow. Surprisingly we waited less than twenty minutes for a table since I thought it would be a complete zoo.
We were momentarily nonplussed to find they charge a sharing fee if each diner doesn't spend $18.95 individually. I had thought Jamie and I could split a Frrrozen Hot Chocolate, but we immediately changed our game plan. I ordered the already exorbitant $19.95 Frrrozen Hot Chocolate, but Jamie went wild and got the $25.95 Insanity Sundae. Say what?
That was crazy impulsive but I respected the chutzpah. "Insanity" was an understatement as it included Southern pecan pie with hot fudge, butter pecan ice cream, caramel sauce, maple walnut topping, toasted almond slices, and a truckload of whipped cream (photo). That was completely nutz. Truly. I stuck with the hot chocolate and also received a mountain of whipped cream.
Was it all that? I think the Frrrozen Hot Chocolate was genuinely satisfying because it was more dessert than drink and not surprisingly overly sweet. The Insanity Sundae was a case study in muchness. Much too muchness. It became an exercise in dissection to make it at all palatable for human consumption. Much of it ended up melting on the saucer holding the sundae.
FOOD - 3 stars...the Frrrozen Hot Chocolate was 4 stars but the Insanity Sundae was insane
AMBIANCE - 3.5 stars...looks like the old-fashioned parlor it always looks like
SERVICE - 3.5 stars...kinda on automatic 
TOTAL - 3 stars...the '3' was more accurate than I expected
RELATED - Hungry in NYC? Here's my colleague of places I've tried and reviewed: 
https://bit.ly/2YS9ELs

I brought my family to NYC for the first time this weekend for a QUICK 2 day trip so it was a touristy weekend to say the least. Obviously I had to make reservation here because it's iconic & fun. Only to be disappointed that we wasted our last dinner.
*Service was NOT good
Barely even greeted at the table
When we sat down, we honestly just wanted to get up and go eat somewhere else but we decided to stay to try the hot coco for my little sister and give it a fair shot.
*The place was gross feeling. Like the whole thing needed a DEEP cleaning. Years of grime & dirt everywhere. The table was sticky & the menus were falling apart.
For a $26 cheeseburger, how can you have your menus looking and feeling like something I don't even want to touch?
I had been here 6 years ago and there was magic. I felt no magic anymore. Just some servers that didn't care to be there & food that looked like it was microwaved. 
I'm not usually one to write bad reviews but I want to save someone else the time & money in NYC to go to a way better restaurant when there are millions of great ones!!

Name: Joe A.
Comment:
All I can say is, "WOW".
I've heard about SERENDIPITY III but you really have to go there and experience it to truly enjoy this treasure.
Eclectic.  Colorful.  Fascinating.  There are not enough positive adjectives to describe SERENDIPITY.  
And, after you absorb the beautiful decor, you get to the main event - the food!
The portions are nothing short of mammoth.  And delicious.  Mac & Cheese with real bacon bits.  Hamburgers that weigh in at least at a pound.  French fries.  You name the culinary treat and SERENDIPITY has it and serves it very well.
But it is the sweets, treats and desserts where SERENDIPITY stands apart from the rest.  Their ice creamsI  That creamy whipped creamI  Those delicious chocolate sprinkles and shavingsI 
And if you think the food servings are generous, the desserts are nothing short of epic in size (and taste).
A bit loud (well, when children see it (and it is kid friendly) they get playful - who could blame them?).  But that does not diminish from the fantastic cuisine, desserts and atmosphere found at SERENDIPITY III.
PS - if you can, have Randy for your server.  The only thing as good as the food there is Randy's sense of fun.

Name: Ramona G.
Comment:
Serendipity 3 is another NY institution that was high on my bucket list. I remember first hearing about this place and their frozen hot chocolate from my sister years ago. We were in Las Vegas and she wanted to visit their location at Caesar's Palace because of the 2001 movie "Serendipity" starring John Cusack and Kate Beckinsale. I only watched the movie a couple of years ago but I have been wanting to visit the original shop in New York.
My cousin and I headed here for dessert one evening. We were super lucky that we were actually seated at the same table John Cusack and Kate Beckinsale sat at in the movie. It's located upstairs in front of the fireplace. 
This place is famous for their frozen hot chocolate that even Jackie O. was a fan, it's said that she even asked for the recipe for a party she was throwing, the owner declined but offered to make it for her. The recipe has since been released to the public.
I ordered their S'mores Frrrozen Hot Chocolate $21.95. It's pretty good but it's a lot. It's too much for a person to finish by themselves. However, they do have a minimum purchase of $18.95 per person, so my cousin had to order his own dessert. I do feel a bit bad for the food waste, since in no way did either of us get through even half of our desserts. I'd suggest maybe one person ordering a dessert and the other order something savory to balance it out. 
Overall a cute ice cream shop. They do have a mini gift shop near their host stand at the front for souvenir purchases.

Comment:
Stopped into Serendipity 3 for dessert and it was well worth the 20 minute wait. This eatery has been a mainstay in NYC, since 1954, and many celebrities make sure they enjoy their goodness. It is A-L-W-A-Y-S packed and full of the sweet loudness of happy  children and content customers. 
We started with the General Tso's Cauliflower, which was amazing. The sweetness that enveloped each piece of cauliflower, combined with the bed of crunchy noodles, made this a perfect selection. 
Then, it was onto their signature item.... the Frrrozen Hot Chocolate. It is served in a LARGE sundae bowl and it is spiritually delightful. Please, don't try to consume it alone. This is a sharable dessert and ask for various spoons. 
Don't hesitate to come into Serendipity 3. You'll enjoy everything from a full meal to one of the best desserts in NYC. Ohhhhhhh, if you want to splurge, you can easily arrange it, on their Guinness Book Of World Record menu. Please, view my pic, for pricing and items.

They told us it would be about an hour and a half, and then we were texted within 15 minutes, which was a little bit of a problem for us because we left the area expecting we'd have much more time. I tried to call to let them know that we were our way, but I could not get through to somebody on their phone, it was just an automated phone menu. They were able to see us when we made it back though, so that was good! The hostesses in our waitress were friendly. Tables were a little bit close together for my liking, so it felt a little bit crowded. 
If you are into over-the-top desserts, I would definitely recommend checking this place at once!

Comment:
If you are a bougie broad like me or just enjoy very nice, Princess-like settings, I definitely recommend coming here. It is one of the cutest restaurants in New York and has a very comfortable and welcoming field to it. The staff is also super friendly and there's great service all around.
I first came here when I was 16 back in 2016 after hearing about this restaurant on the cult classic "gossip girl". I remember loving the Sundays and the overall experience. This was before I was vegan though. Coming back I was pretty disappointed at the lack of vegan dessert options especially because there are so many vegan dinner options. I really wanted to indulge in a Sunday or something of the sorts but there was literally nothing outside of the frozen hot chocolate. They could at least have one dairy free ice cream flavor...!
On top of that, this place has gotten ridiculously expensive. My friend paid eight dollars for a freaking Coke (see our receipt). Her burger was almost $30!!!!
I loved my vegan truffle mac & cheese. It was honestly one of the best vegan mac & cheese is that I've ever had. However, my mom did not like her Sunday nor does she like the cauliflower that she got. I tried the cauliflower and you could taste better. It was very under done. Also they only gave four pieces of cauliflower and it was $18. Make that make sense.
It was also a bit dirty... dust everywhere, floaties in the cup, tables not wiped properly...
I would love to come here again in the future but they need some updates and operation fixes.

Name: Steve M.
Comment:
Opposite google in what felt like a fairly run down area of Manhattan (we walked through Chelsea to get there and that was a down and out section)
The place inside is very nice if a little dark 
Walking downstairs is super cool
The food and drinks are very expensive for the small portion sizes. We had a group of 6 and a number of starters and mains
I recall the tuna tartar spring rolls - about the smallest I've ever seen and not that tasty
Short rib and scallion pancake was nice (again a few mouthfuls)
Chili rock shrimp was nice ( didn't seem to have any chili in it?) and felt like a slightly bigger portion size - the waiter initially forget this one so we had with our mains
Sichuan pork dumplings - don't even try to compare to din tai fung or a dumpling or place - pretty much zero flavor
We had a couple of other appetizers 
We had black cod main - nice but about 4 mouthfuls ;-)
Seabass - whole small fish and best avoided
Broken chili chicken - not memorable 
Think we had a couple of other dishes 
I had 2 glasses of red wine - I noticed after I checked the bill I was charged for 3...
It felt a bit like a conveyor belt once the food started coming
Food ok - portions too small for the price
Alcohol a bit pricey even for Manhattan  - especially when you're overcharged!
Service was ok - no personal touch 
Not a place I would recommend based on my review - at best I'd give this a 2/5 (closer to a 1.5) for the overall feel but let down by small overpriced and in some cases tasteless food
Our whole party felt the same - I still recall when we had our mains and the general comment was 'is this an appetizer!!'
Take note - best avoided

Name: Nikki C.
Comment:
The atmosphere was fantastic, it was gorgeous and dimly lit. When you walk in, you'll feel like you are in a movie, trust me, you'll see what I mean. 
We came with a party of 7 & made reservations for 7:15pm. As we were seated we were greeted promptly and were introduced to the tasting menu & the main menu. We ordered a handful of things; starting off with the edamame dimsum, jade dimsum, short rib & scallion pancake, pork dumplings, lobster egg rolls, the dimsum tower for two; all of these were beautifully decadent & delicious. What grabbed my heart was the short rib scallion pancake & the edamame dimsum, amazing!!! 
For entrees to share, we got the wok noodles with prawns, sausage fried rice, steak (which was heavenly), this beef dish (see picture) that was glistening and so tender. The food was excellent!! I was in love with every single bite I took. 
However, even though the food was subpar, the service we received was quite hostile & rushed. When we ordered drinks, our server was straight on the "I must see your ID if you're drinking alcohol", which was skeptical at first because as I was going to the bathroom and eased drop with a newly seated group behind us, the same server didn't ask them for ID. Perhaps it was because we look younger, and I do respect the legal regulations, but if you do it to us you must do it to others as well. 
We also ordered one dessert in the end and the server abruptly said "Is that it? Just that?" In a hypocritical way. We all felt rushed & disrespected at this point and wanted to leave. 
I'm giving the benefit of the doubt, but aside from that; the food was excellent & believe this can improve vastly.

Name: Keecha H.
Comment:
Grrr...
Buddakan... how did you lose your mojo? 
We used to dine here a few times per quarter. Having not been to the restaurant in a few months, we were excited to come back.  
The word that best describes our server, Monica, is lovely.  She offered valuable insights into the new menu and its layout. 
We stuck with our perennial favorites like the cauliflower and buns while deciding to try out the cold peanut noodles and sweet and sour pork.  
The experience left us with a question: Where is the flavor? None of the dishes had a zing. Each tasted as lifeless as the others. 
Perhaps looking at our faces, the manager Spencer came to the table three times. We were very honest with him the third time- the food was unenjoyable, and we would not be back.  
Here's the word of the day for Spencer: defensive.  It is followed by mansplaining.  He even bothered to tell us that his mother was in recently having enjoyed many of the dishes that we had. 
Relevance?  Huh? 
What ADULT manager does that? Actually what ADULT does that?
Good thing for the restaurant that they are in a prime location. People will flock there unknowingly. As for people who know when a culinary program is off, you will find much better food elsewhere.

Comment:
This was a surprisingly affordable higher end dinner. I came here with a larger party of 12 for a birthday party and our total bill came out to around $500 pre tip. 
I really loved the interior design - the lighting and candles were a unique touch. The service was great as well. My friend found a hair in one of the crispy tofu dishes. They quickly replaced the dish. They also gave us a matcha cheesecake for our friend's birthday! 
The food was good, but there are a couple of dishes to avoid. The Wild Mushroom Chow Fun Noodles tasted like plain cooked noodles. My friends didn't particularly like the Kung Pao Chicken Dumplings - outside is dry and inside isn't flavorful. They also said the Pork Lo Mein didn't taste like much either. A lot of the food just needed a bit more flavor, so we asked for sauce. They gave as the tray of sauces. 
A couple of items stood out! The Crispy Tofu is a must, but you have to eat them quick while they're crispy. My friends really liked the Pork Bao Buns - the meat was sticky & flavorful but also fell apart. The whole table agreed that the Rejuvenation beverage was the best drink overall.

Name: Katherine L.
Comment:
When my husband and I were planning our new york trip, I kept complaining about his decision to make a reservation at Buddakan. I thought it was going to be an over-priced Chinese-American fusion meal like Tao, where it's more about the show and the ambiance than the food. I was wrong. 
While the restaurant very much resembled the ambiance at TAO, I found the food to be much better, and more worth the price tag. I thought the food here were more innovative, and had more interesting flavors, than just making the food in smaller portions, "upscaling" it from typical Chinese food. From my recollection, it wasn't as expensive either. 
While intrigued at their prix fixe menu at first, we ended up opting out of it since it didn't include some of the items we wanted and our friends cannot eat beef. We ordered a la carte and stuffed ourselves while still coming in below the prix fixe menu price. 
Note-worthy were the General Tso's Dumplings where the stuffing encased the soup, Tea Smoked spare ribs for the flavor and tenderness of the meat, and the Lobster Fried Rice. Those were the stars of the dinner. 
The items that we thought fell short were the pork ribs/buns as the buns got soggy, Wok Fried XO Cheung Fun that wasn't flavorful enough and had little to no XO sauce. 
But the dish that sold us was their matcha basque cheesecake topped with crispy strawberry flakes. It was so good that we had to order a second one, and my mouth is watering as I am typing out this review! 
Everything was great here - from the service to the food. I loved the twist they had on standard dishes, and appreciated the innovation to the fusion food rather than just using more expensive ingredients to elevate the experience.

Name: Naomi S.
Comment:
I had dinner here on May 28th. The fish advertised as yellowtail is (allegedly) NOT yellowtail. After eating the Spicy Yellowtail Yu Sheng
(smoked chili, pineapple) I became sick. Yes- ingesting raw fish always means risking illness. However, the type of sickness I had is one you can  only get by eating indigestible wax esters..  I linked the illness below but will spare the disgusting details here. I believe they are allegedly serving oil fish. Knowingly or unknowingly.
Beyond the sickness- the food was subpar. I also had the cod and a lobster egg roll. Would not be surprised if it wasn't actually cod. It did have a weirdly sweet flavor. I wanted to enjoy it but did not find anything particularly amazing about it. Especially when taking the price point into consideration. I Another disappointment was when asking about a wine pairing- the server recommended sauvignon blanc. Against my better judgement I went with the recommendation. Immediately, my suspicion confirmed. The server knew nothing about wine. Not their fault, but do not suggest a random wine. 
https://globalseafoods.com/a/s/blogs/news/escolar-it-is-no-accident
https://www.sushi-pedia.com/en/sushi/aburasokomutsu/

Name: La Deana S.
Comment:
Refreshingly Different! 
This restaurant is a gem. Dined here for an early dinner (arrived at 5, when they opened). The Chili Rock Shrimp were ok, but the most prevalent taste was that of hot sauce (not my favorite). For the main course, I chose the Glazed Alaskan Black Cod (recommended by our server). It was great. This fish was lightly season with a slightly sweet glaze and was served with garlic eggplant. I thoroughly enjoyed that combination, as I'm a big fan of eggplant. We paired it with the Dungeness Crab Sticky Rice (also recommended) as well as the Lobster Fried Rice. The Crab Sticky rice was surprisingly tasteful (I've had a lot of bland rice). The Lobster was good, but not as flavorful. Finished off the meal with the Ube Mochi Donuts - another pleasant surprise! These donuts were sweet, but not overly so, and were nicely paired with a crème and sliced bananas. 
Minus one star for ambiance. It's a lovely restaurant (in terms of location and decor), but the tables are placed so close together, it was a little uncomfortable once the restaurant started to fill up. I understand it, just didn't enjoy it. Regardless, the food was well worth it. 
Lastly, the server was kind and accommodating - well done!

The delicate dumpling and soft filling combined with the perfect broth is *chef's kiss*. Do not miss out on the Charred Filet of Beef or the Lobster Fried Rice.. TO DIE FOR. The ambiance is one of a kind! Sprawling staircase, massive chandelier and waxy candelabras. The staff is phenomenal and will answer all of your questions. You should definitely have a drink at the bar in front while waiting to be seated. This is my death row meal.

Name: Bunny L.
Comment:
One of the best dining everyplace I've had in Manhattan. 
The ambiance is beautiful with theatrical lighting and a grand, dramatic dining hall. Smaller private rooms are off to the rides with cozy corners for smaller parties. 
The food is spectacular! Delicious quality and unique Asian fusion. Excellent quality, taste and so much to pick from. 
The service was excellent and have returned several times. This place is great for an intimate date or a special celebration.

Name: Daniel G.

It was striking at first, but the more you look the more you realize it's not nice but just dark. We tried about 1/3 of the menu. While some of the food is tasty, it's lacking the quality you'd expect from the prices. Some of the drinks were good but again, unoriginal.  The sesame infused mezcal drink tasted like lo mien. There's definitely nicer places to go eat in the city for the price they are charging here. Don't fall into the hype.

The bar is NOT a place to eat at, there is no leg room under the bar to put your knees and the bar stools are incredibly uncomfortable.. which tells me this is the spot people wait for a table, but still - not comfortable whatsoever. 
The staff is very knowledgeable of what is gluten free and vegan. I had the Wild Mushroom Chow Fun and it was DELICIOUS!!! I could eat it everyday of my life. 
The place is very dark and sexy inside. Great for a first date or anniversary date. 
Although I saw many business partner dinners happening while there both times. 
I would definitely come back but likely eat at a table next time - womp womp. 
The cocktail menu is fabulous and the cocktails even better!

Name: Shirin B.
Comment: We recently celebrated at Buddakan with a party of nine, and I must commend their exceptional handling of food allergies. My son has life-threatening allergies to all nuts and seeds and we were concerned especially since many dishes at the restaurant contain sesame oil and seeds. However, our worries were quickly alleviated when Chef Zachary met with us with a menu and highlighter in hand. He sat down with us, took meticulous notes of my son's allergies, and highlighted all the dishes my son could safely enjoy. His knowledge of the menu and ingredients was impressive and detailed, providing me with a level of comfort I've seldom felt in restaurants concerning food allergies. My son could indulge without any concerns and that made the meal so enjoyable for us all.  I highly recommend Buddakan for their delicious food, fantastic ambiance and their flawless system that ensures a safe and enjoyable meal from server to chef and back to the customer.

Our party had a variety of food options with very delicious taste to them. My favorite was the pepper steak and duck salad. Comment:
My takeaway of two things from Buddakan - one the best and one may need improvement 
Edmame dumplings - the softest, the creamiest, the most delicious dumpling you would ever have! Ugh craving for it right now honestly, and it's only 7:30 am. They came well steamed, well cooked and the outer shell was so soft I could cry. I will most certainly come back for it.

Name: Bowie W.
Comment:
AMBIENCE: (4/5)
- Interesting decor concept, some Renaissance art mixed with Asian fusion decor?
- Loud music blasting makes it a bit hard to hear your dining partner almost like you're at a table in the club but overall the place looks very upscale. There's a huge set of staircase leading to another level of tables. 
SERVICE: (5/5)
- Took a while to be seated even though we had reservations. No idea how it works since we were asked to wait along with a large group of people also waiting at the main area but somehow the waitress was able to identify us
- Waiter came by & was very pleasant, had time to ask us how we were liking things during the dinner even though it was very busy
- Food & drinks came at a great pace
FOOD/DRINKS: (4/5)
- Oxtail Dumplings (3/5): Honestly a disappointment. The idea was so promising. The dumpling skin was nice and thin but the oxtail inside was a bit mushy. It urned something to give it texture. The taste of the oxtail was alright, not as strong of oxtail flavor as we'd like. 
- Tea Smoked Spare Ribs (4/5): falls-off-the-bone tender as it should be. Although the hoisin barbeque was a bit too tart for my liking but that's a personal taste. 
- Crispy Tofu (4/5): Each side is nice & crispy, the inside hot & steaming. A hint of spicy from the sweet glaze. Pairs great with rice. 
- Tuna Tartare Spring Rolls (5/5): So so delicious. The skin was so crunchy, the tartare perfectly seasoned with a kick of the crispy shallot. Reminds me of the Tuna Tartare Cigars at the famous Crustaceans in Beverly Hills. 
- Halaya (4.5/5): Ube and pineapple was a surprising combo. Ube could've been stronger but I could at least taste it, which is better than other ube cocktails I've tried.
- Jade (3/5): Good drink, a bit of a refresher & pallet cleanser due to the cucumber. Wished the matcha was a bit stronger. The pandan I couldn't really taste at all but most pandan drinks always seem to fall short. 
OVERVIEW: (4/5)
- Must have food: tuna tartare spring rolls
- Must have drink: halaya
- Great ambience, loud up beat music to get you in the mood to start the night & a promising cocktail menu. Perfect place to grab a drink during happy hour to start the night. The food was subpar for the price so save your stomach but I would def order the spring rolls as an app

My email address is: dragana.bjelica@starr-restaurant.com

Dragana Bjelica
General Manager

Name: Hulgrid G.
Comment: Went for a girlfriend of mine's birthday and the atmosphere is fabulous. It's forgiving if you wear a revealing outfit, or get an accidental smudge on pants and be fine because it is so very dark. Now the food: everything was 10/10. The lamb chops, peppered steak, oxtail dumplings, Chinese sausage fried rice, and their version of bang-bang shrimp was all prepared and seasoned perfectly. We ordered an array of dishes to share and you should too if you visit: Do not rob yourself of only trying 1-2 items here. I will add that getting the kimchi rice was a nice break from the meat and oily dishes we got; it was light and the kimchi still had a nice bite to it. Overall, fantastic vibes, service was great and I can say the food will have me coming back for a random night of amazing food. By the way, with all that we ordered (split 4 ways, 1 cocktail each and gratuity included) our bill was 90/person.

Name: Deeps S.
Comment: I loved the look and feel of this restaurant- Buddakan is a high end Asian restaurant in Chelsea. We had reservations but were running late - staff was really helpful in pushing out our reservation and accomodating us later. We got seated in one of their inner dining rooms but i loved their main area - dark, high celings with lovely decor. We started with cocktails - Blush, Jade, and. dragon fruit cocktail. I  liked the Blush (lychee based), skip the Cucumber based one - was not good. For appetizers and mains, we for the edamame dumplings, the mapo tofu, the eggplant tofu, and the crispy tofu. The mapo tofu was great - not too spicy, others on the table thought it could use more flavor/have more of a sichuan taste and I felt the same but overall it was good. The crispy tofu was a table favorite- crisp and sweet - an interesting combo. The eggplant tofu was decent - the pineapple and cashews that were added made it unique. For dessert, we got the matcha semifreddo - this had a clementine sorbet on top with blondie crumbles - really delicious dessert with a gorgeous presentation. The dessert was the highlight for sure.  Would come back for drinks and dessert - entrees were decent if you arent craving authentic Asian food. Great ambiance - so good place for drinks

Name: Carina H.
Comment:
I've dined at Buddakan three times in the past two years, but my first two times were for work events. I've been seated at the iconic long table, a smaller table to the side of the long table, and this time in another room to the left of the stairs. The elegant and mysterious vibe of the restaurant has remained consistent. Not one thing I've had at this restaurant has disappointed either, although some dishes were more standout than others.
The dim sum tasting is a unique experience, since as a dim sum lover, I am only used to traditional dim sum flavors. The dim sum served here has its own twist on the original dim sum, and it's fun trying each of the dim sum here one-by-one with the rest of your party. I wouldn't say anything is particularly standout for me from this order, besides the shrimp toast because I generally love shrimp dim sum. One thing I would like to note is that the edamame dumplings taste way better after dipping it in the soy sauce.
Oh, the Kung Pao Monkfish and the Dungeoness Crab Sticky Rice. I didn't order the Lobster Fried Rice this time around so I could try the crab sticky rice, and it did not disappoint. All three dishes: the fish, sticky rice, and fried rice live in my head now.
If you love fried fish, you'll love the monkfish. It's crispy, and the flavor is just chef's kiss. The monkfish and crab sticky rice pair really well together too, since both are seafood dishes. I usually don't eat crab (not my preference for meat), but I loved this dish and even the crab part of it. The flavor went so well paired with the XO sauce.
I also added an additional comment about an inside joke anniversary on our OpenTable reservation (the Sunday before Valentine's Day), and the staff did not disappoint. (They also added candles and a little anniversary chocolate on our dessert that we ordered.) The waiter was friendly, funny, and helpful with dish recommendations.

Name: Carlos P.
Comment:
This is definitely a happening place in NYC, and very Instagramable. 
The food is good to decent, although certain dishes like the lobster fried rice are a far stretch from the typical fried rice in all aspects of an interpretation, from texture, to ingredients, to flavor - if you ask me, skip it. Although, it's not horrible; just lacks flavor for the price. 
The other dishes we tried were from good to great: the pork potstickers were delicious without any sauce as the somewhat greasy pork flavor (in a flavorful way) came shining through - that dish was true to life in how you'd find it in your typical Chinese restaurant. 
The Tea Smoked Spare Ribs were also very good and if you don't eat much you can have them as your entree, especially if you can also munch on something else. 
My friend had the Pea Shoot Salad app/side, which looked like yucky seaweed mush, but then again, I'm not into that kind of stuff with funky flavors, and at $20 dollars for a side, I'd say unless you are a hard core veggie, it's not worth it - she said it was very 'rich' - whatever that means; I took as very strong tasting - and for me, say no more, I'll stay away since veggies and green things 'truly' make me sick in the gut. 
La piece de resistance was the Chocolate Malted Tart dessert - totally worth it, but it's a tiny portion, so not enough for 2 - you are gonna have to pay the $18 per serving/person. It's their take on a lava cake, but with a toasty cake surround and super sweet and rich flowy warm chocolate fudge inside. 
Buddakan is definitely an experience I recommend when traversing the big apple; just remember to make reservations, specially if you want a spot at the communal main dining room with all the beautiful chandeliers.

Buddakan is going to become a standard of mine and my family as we visit the city, and a highly recommended destination dinner for anyone visiting the area. Chelsea is becoming our favorite neighborhood in the city, and Buddakan tops off even a day trip to town. If you are coming for the tasting menu, give yourself time. My initial review dinged the restaurant and staff for shortcomings partially attributed to my own faults during our first visit. Both dinners reveal such a broad palate of flavors. Buddakon provides both a swanky and comfortable environment while dishing out some of the best Asian cuisine we've tasted...ever. We're becoming addicted, and we look forward to our next visit. We'll likely plan our next city trip around it.

Name: Elena W.
Comment:
3.5 stars rounded up to 4. I was fortunate enough to be treated to dinner here at Buddakan on Columbia University's dime and it was really good for the most part!
Right when walking in to the restaurant, you'll notice that it's SO dark inside lol. There's a complimentary coat check at the front desk which is really convenient. While you're waiting to be seated there's like a bar/lounge area. Then when you get to your table it's still dark as hell lol, but guess that's the vibe they're going for. When you use the bathrooms here, it's so hilarious, it's even hella dark inside the stalls lol with the black toilets too.
About the food, I generally enjoyed the appetizers more than the entrees. The Cantonese spring rolls and tuna tartare spring rolls were amazing and the edamame and general tso's dumplings were also great! The only stand out dish among the entrees was the sweet and crispy jumbo shrimp. I also am confused on their black pepper beef dish; the description said it includes crispy bird's nest, but it was unclear to me and my friends whether it was actual bird's nest (swallow spit) or just regular batter fried in a way to make it decorative. Dessert was fine, I liked the three little tarts better than the mochi doughnuts. 
Concluding thoughts: cool place for young locals to eat. If you're coming to NYC on vacay, I don't think this spot was so spectacular that it warrants a visit on your limited time here. This is not a restaurant to bring your older Asian parents lol they will complain about the darkness and loud music. Also unclear if this restaurant is disability friendly because we had to use stairs to go from the bar/lounge down to the floor where all the tables were. Maybe they do have an elevator and it was just too damn dark to see it lol

Comment: Came here on a Monday night for a birthday dinner. Overall, the service was exceptionally slow. We got there for 7 PM reservation and didn't leave until nearly 10 PM. It was not due to the fact that we were taking your sweet time. It was solely because the food was coming out at snails pace. We pretty much got almost everything on the menu so the bill was not cheap by any means. It's not like I did not try everything. The edamame steamed dumplings are good but not worth the cost. The tuna rolls as appetizers are $20 for just three small little nothing taquitos. They're good but not worth the price tag. We also had the Peking duck several different fried rice and the spare ribs. All super average the chocolate cake wasn't really that good it's probably one of the better things on the menu though. I did very much like the coconut mango dessert because it was more traditional Asian. The restaurant is also super dark which I think can add to the ambience but instead it distracted from it. The cocktails seem to be good but we ended up getting a bottle Chandon. Overall, it was fine food but to be honest you're looking for good Asian food I would just go to either Koreatown Chinatown or Fort Lee New Jersey.

Name: Christi G.
Comment: SO DISAPPOINTED!  I took my boyfriend here for his birthday in mid December.  I have been to Buddakan several times and it is one of my favorite restaurants.  Unfortunately, this visit did not live up to my prior experiences.  I made a reservation for a party of six for my boyfriend's birthday.  We arrived early and sat in the bar area for pre-dinner cocktails with friends.  Our entire party had arrived by the reservation time.  Unfortunately, we had to wait 45 minutes after the reservation time to be seated. We checked with the front desk many times and told them where we were sitting.  The final time I went to the front desk the woman told me the person was in the bar to seat us.  I had seen her come in about 5 minutes prior to look for us but despite having told the front desk multiple times where we were sitting, I had to hunt her down and tell her we were the party she needed to seat.  The woman up front apologized and offered to send some deserts to us for the wait.  After being seated we chose the tasting menu.  The first course came fairly quickly but the second round took another 45 minutes to be served after the first round. Our waiter did not check on us to advise why it was taking so long.  He also came by towards the end of the meal while carrying a large stack of napkins - his end of shift duties.  I worked in fine dining and that is a no no!  With an 8:15 reservation, we did not finish until 11:30.  I was incredibly disappointed.  To add insult to injury there were no "free" desserts as offered by the woman at the front or any compensation for the poor service.  The only bright spot was that the food was as delicious as I remembered.  Unfortunately overshadowed by the poor service.

Name: Julianna Y.
Comment:
Service: 4/5 
Great service, slightly a bit slow but everyone was accommodating. Just a fair warning if you have a reservation, you still might have to wait at least 20 mins for your table. 
What we ordered: 
Edamame Dumplings: 3/5 
So these are delicious, however I feel like they're lacking more flavor. The texture is chewy and delicious, tastes more like mashed potatoes dumplings. 
Chili Rock Shrimp: 4/5 
Decent amount, has a slight lack in flavor. Was expecting there to be a slight kick in the sauce, however there is nothing spicy about it. Paired this up with a side sauce that I ordered in addition where they mixed what seemed like chili paste and some Thai sweet chili. Would highly recommend asking for those sauces if you order this dish! 
Mongolian Lamb: 5/5 
Lamb was cooked perfectly!! The sauce added was also delicious. Tasted like a sweet garlic. The bokchoy was also good, just interesting they chopped off the leafy part that most people consume. 
Tuna tar tar spring rolls: 4/5 
Delicious, fish tasted fresh, and paired so nicely with the   crunchy spring roll. 
Crying Chocolate: 5/5 
I'm not a huge dessert fan, but this was exquisite! I loved that the lava cake was paired with Vietnamese coffee, and the coffee crumble worked so well with one another. Would order again! So many unique textures and flavors combined with each other. 
Ambiance: 5/5 
Beautifully decorated ambiance. Didn't get a chance to sit downstairs which was brighter and prettier, but perhaps next time when I have a bigger party. The vase shaped doors/rooms were also a nice touch

About a 1-1.5: I first tried out Buddakan in Philadelphia for a work trip, so I kind of knew what to expect in terms of the food.  
We came here for an early dinner, making advance reservations; it's good to do that, as this place does get packed quickly.
We had the following to share:
+Jade Shrimp Dumpling - with bamboo shoot and pea shoots; these were just okay; the dumpling skin seemed to have been oversteamed and super sticky
+General Tso's Dumplings - with chicken and hot & sour broth; was not fan of the hot & sour broth
+Oxtail Dumplings - with sweet soy, green apple, and jalapenos; this was a bit more on the sweeter side, rather than savory
+Tuna Tartare Spring Rolls - with crispy shallot and ponzu
+Spicy Yellowtail Yu Sheng - with smoked chili and pineapple; not sure if the smoked chili was necessary in this dish
+Chili Rock Shrimp - with toasted ginger and pineapple
+the Glazed Alaskan Black Cod - with chili eggplant and black bean relish; the eggplant was not much of a complement; the black code seemed to be slightly undercooked
+Sweet & Crispy Jumbo Shrimp - with citrus salad, radish, and candied walnuts
+Lobster Fried Rice - with saffron and cilantro; the saffron was a bit too overpowering in this dish
+Dungeness Crab Sticky Rice - with X.O. sauce and Chinese broccoli; this was just okay; could have had a bit more crab meat
+and, Kimchi & Shrimp Fried Rice - double deluxe soy and garlic chips; would need extra soy sauce to amp up the flavor
I think our experience at the Philly location was slightly better as well as in terms of the flavor of the food.
Service was fine, but slightly pushy - we thought we had ordered a good variety, but they really wanted us to order additional drinks and dishes, stating that our group would find it 'not enough' - it was a little obnoxious that it was kept being brought up each time were were checked in.
+inconsistent food items
+pushy service

Name: Jonah M.
Comment:
The food here is very good- the service here was a little bit like a circus. Upon sitting it took the waiter about 10 minutes to come take any drink orders. We tried to put in our appetizer and we were told the chef likes to get the whole order so he can course it out which... did not happen. 
We had someone set food on our table we did not order walk away, come back, and then pick that dish up and serve it to another table, which is a very big no-no in the restaurant business. 
After we ordered our food it came out sporadic, not coursed. two appetizers came out one first then 5 minutes later another. then one entree came out with a bowl of rice. Quickly followed by another entree, then 5 minutes later the third. 
I did not realize this place is sort of family style, which I like, since I get to try all the food. However, everything we ordered was fried (which I didn't realize), and though the flavor was good the weirdness or service had me feeling pretty weird. 
Overall the dishes were fun and unique the atmosphere was dark and romantic and there is a pop culture aspect to the place as it was in Sex and the City or something. Very interesting experience, I'm not sure if I would go again.
