Name: Ezra C.
Comment:
In the neighborhood of Midwood, Brooklyn, Di Fara Pizza has earned a reputation that has resounded far beyond the city's limits, gaining acclaim as one of the best pizzerias not just in New York, but in the entire United States. For over half a century, this family-run institution has been churning out pizzas that transcend the realm of comfort food, transforming a humble slice into an art form.
The magic of Di Fara lies in its commitment to quality, tradition, and simplicity. Each pizza is a careful symphony of flavors, featuring a perfect balance of mozzarella and Parmigiano-Reggiano cheeses, a tangy yet sweet tomato sauce, and a sprinkle of fresh basil. The crust is a masterpiece in its own right - crispy on the outside, chewy on the inside, with a hint of smokiness from the oven.
Of the many pies available, the classic round and square pizzas shine the brightest. The round pizza, my personal favorite, is a perfect example of a traditional New York-style pie, with its thin, crisp yet foldable crust, and a perfectly balanced layer of cheese and sauce. The square pie, also known as the Sicilian, boasts a thick, doughy crust and an abundance of cheese and toppings, a more substantial yet equally delicious alternative.
Aside from the pizza, the menu at Di Fara is brief, yet every item is curated with the same attention to detail. The meatball sub and the lasagna are noteworthy, each packed with homemade flavor and offering a delightful divergence from the pizza-centric experience.
However, what truly sets Di Fara Pizza apart is not just the food but the entire dining experience. From placing your order at the counter to watching your pizza being prepared and baked in the vintage oven, the process is as much a part of the experience as the meal itself. This sense of tradition is palpable in every inch of the pizzeria, from the vintage photos adorning the walls to the worn-out tiles underfoot.
The service at Di Fara is as authentic as the pizza itself. During busy hours, patience is required as each pizza is crafted with care and not rushed, despite the growing queue. While some might view this as a drawback, to me, it's a testament to Di Fara's commitment to quality. It's not fast food; it's an artisanal experience.
The ambiance of Di Fara is unpretentious and homely, a stark contrast to the flashy, modern eateries that often populate New York's culinary scene. The dining room is small and unassuming, with a few tables and chairs scattered around. Yet, the aroma of baking dough, simmering sauce, and melting cheese that fills the air creates a warm and inviting atmosphere. The bustling noise of the kitchen, the occasional clang of the pizza cutter, and the lively chatter of diners all contribute to the lively, almost nostalgic, ambiance that is distinctly Di Fara's.
Di Fara Pizza is a testament to the beauty of simplicity, tradition, and uncompromising quality. With its exceptional pizza, nostalgic ambiance, and the heartfelt service that comes from a family-run business, it is a beloved institution that has firmly rooted itself in the heart of Brooklyn and in the hearts of pizza lovers everywhere. It's more than a pizzeria; it's a slice of New York history that continues to serve memories one pizza at a time.

Name: Benjamin E.
Comment:
Was in the neighborhood for lunch and being an out of towner, I always here that NYC pizza beats them all, so I decided to try out some NYC Pizza.  
From the looks of the articles on the wall, this place has a lot of history of being one of NYC's top pizza parlors.
I got the 2 Cheese Slice lunch combo that comes with a water or soda. 
Perhaps it's because in my mind when I think about a NY slice of pizza, I am imagining a huge slice of cheezy goodness.  However, my dreams we quickly dashed when I got these  2 slices.  In my mind I thought to myself 
"Where's the rest of it" ....."I know people are talking about the inflation crisis....but wow....."
The texture is definitely different from other types of pizza that I have had, where there is a nice chewy top and a crunchy bottom.  I definitely love the texture.  However with regards to the taste, it might have been good but, I really couldn't tell as the bottom was over cooked and burnt, so I mostly tasted the bitter char of the dough. I had brought it back to the casher and she was willing to give me 2 fresh slices, but when she lifted the pizza, the rest of the pizza looked no better and I had not time to wait for a new pizza to be made. (Nor was that an option given to me.)
I had sat outside to try to enjoy my lunch and take in the view on NYC, but was met with a powerful smelling oder of urine and I was also pestered by a homeless man asking for money and though I offered to go and buy him a pizza, he declined and wanted cash. (I didn't give him money).....I guess in some case, I did get to take in the NYC view.......

Name: Mike C.
Comment:
Bourdain.
This name caught my eye when a local food consolidator announced this east coast pizzeria offered their food delivered to me.  No, it was not your standard call and deliver purchase as they offered a variety of frozen pies to be delivered on a recent Saturday.  Locale's ad (
https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=eV14YscMnfCvN6yKPtbXiQ&userid=saJuyv784apZzH--psHZ3Q)
 made this a must.
There were three or four choices.  I thought the meatball parmesan pie (
https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=9ANazPyxmj7Wr1a4M_dbSQ&userid=saJuyv784apZzH--psHZ3Q)
 sounded interesting.  Kudos for the packaging (
https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=7ruRjmoKcmdtA43cBwgmTw&userid=saJuyv784apZzH--psHZ3Q)
 and a nice surprise with basil and parmesan cheese in the paper wrapping.  I cooked my frozen pie as instructed and had half of it for lunch, three slices.  Being previously frozen, the crust shined for me, thin and cracker like without being hard and dry.  The toppings didn't stand out likely due to the sauce and cheese drying out from being frozen.  I thawed and layed the basil leaves to add another flavor (
https://www.yelp.com/user_local_photos?select=6oJa7na1HTf-KxzyYTMkrw&userid=saJuyv784apZzH--psHZ3Q).
  Heck, they even packaged the parmesan to sprinkle over the pie.  Now that's putting the effort in.  
My review probably doesn't do this business justice as I'm sure fresh pies made and eaten there won't have the issues mentioned.  I hope to make it to NYC and Brooklyn in the near future at some point.  I'm treating this event as a preview to future enjoyable experiences.
